_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised autopilot, LOCAL track)
_meta.note: FL locals batch — Orlando D3 (Chapin) + St. Petersburg Mayor (Welch) and council D5/D6/D7 (Figgs-Sanders, Driscoll, Givens). Roster verified June 2026. Scores reset to null where no evidence (prior speculative party-default FALSEs cleared); FALSE only where a documented vote/statement/endorsement exists; TRUE where documented alignment. Surfacing high-value deviations: Welch's open Christian (COGIC) faith + maintained 287(g); Givens's ordained-minister status.

profile.background: City Commissioner, District 3, City of Orlando — sworn Jan 12, 2026 after Dec 9, 2025 runoff (succeeded 20-year incumbent Robert Stuart). Career at Darden Restaurants and Mears Transportation (rose to EVP); now owns Chapin Communications. Son of former Orange County Mayor Linda Chapin (D). Prior boards: OUC, Downtown Development Board (chair), MPB, Dr. Phillips Center oversight.

roger-chapin.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: City Commissioner District 3, sworn Jan 12, 2026 (orlando.gov). Orlando council races are formally nonpartisan; Chapin is a registered Democrat who positioned as the 'govern from the middle' candidate against a progressive in the Dec 2025 runoff (Florida Politics). He was endorsed by Fraternal Order of Police Orlando Lodge 25, Orlando Professional Firefighters Local 1365, and Orange County Sheriff John Mina personally — but per the LOCAL public_justice q0 bar (affirmative advocacy / running a police-partnership program), campaign endorsements alone aren't enough to score TRUE on a brand-new commissioner with no votes yet; that cell left null. Platform is municipal-services / land-use / transit / OUC-focused; he has avoided social-issue commentary, telling reporters he wants to keep 'partisan politics out of city hall.' NO EVIDENCE FOUND on abortion, marriage, schools, election administration, immigration, self-defense, or christian-liberty cells — those cells null, not penalized. No church/faith affiliation in any bio. Confidence set to roster_verified (real official, ~5 months in seat, no scoreable positions yet beyond endorsements).

profile.religion: Ordained Baptist Minister; member of Suncoast Church (St. Petersburg); member of the Baptist Ministers Conference of St. Petersburg & Vicinity
profile.education: MA Christian Ministry, Bethune-Cookman University; BA Mass Communications, USF St. Petersburg; AA, Florida State University; Lakewood HS (Senior Class President)
profile.background: City Council, District 7, St. Petersburg — sworn Jan 2, 2025 (defeated Wengay Newton Nov 2024). Fourth-generation St. Pete native; educator and community activist; serves on Pinellas Schools ICROC and CONA. Term to Jan 2029.

corey-givens-jr.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 7 St. Petersburg City Council, sworn Jan 2, 2025 (stpete.org). HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION: Givens is an ordained Baptist Minister, member of Suncoast Church and the Baptist Ministers Conference — christian_liberty q0 TRUE on his official city bio. HARD evidence on the LGBTQ+ cells: voted with the Oct 9, 2025 majority advancing the 'Harmony' (Pride colors) + 'History' flag resolution, telling colleagues the council should 'flex our muscles and use our voices' (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE; WUSF, St. Pete Catalyst). Publicly defends city DEI funding and St. Pete Pride against DeSantis preemption ('create a culture war that doesn't exist'), explicitly questioning the 'merit/excellence vs. diversity/equity' framing — family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE. On immigration: promotes the FDLE 'suspected unauthorized alien encounters' dashboard as a transparency / accountability tool to track and limit ICE cooperation ('After what happened in Minneapolis, we want to make sure that does not happen here'), positioning with residents and faith leaders calling on St. Pete to end its 287(g) ICE partnership — border_immigration q1 FALSE, q2 FALSE (WTSP, Fox 13). Voted NO on FY26 budget but reason was wanting MORE spending on youth programs ($3M was 'a drop in the bucket'); does NOT satisfy fiscal-restraint rubric — economic_stewardship q2 null. No documented direct statements on abortion, marriage definition, election administration, or 2A — those cells null. (Flag: Oct 2025 council clash with Driscoll over a Lincoln Cemetery social-media post; 2024 campaign-finance flag — $500 candidate-check deposited to personal account, attributed to mistake per Tampa Bay Times.)

4.note: Voted with majority Oct 9, 2025 to advance the 'Harmony' (Pride) and 'History' city-flag resolution; defended city support for St. Pete Pride against state DEI restrictions.

2.note: Defended DEI / Pride city funding against DeSantis preemption; explicitly criticized the 'merit, excellence and intelligence vs. diversity, inclusion and equity' framing.

0.note: Ordained Baptist Minister; member of Suncoast Church and the Baptist Ministers Conference of St. Petersburg & Vicinity — public faith profession on official city bio.

1.note: Promotes FDLE 'suspected unauthorized alien encounters' dashboard as transparency tool to track and limit ICE enforcement in St. Pete ('After what happened in Minneapolis…').

2.note: Aligns with residents/faith leaders calling on St. Pete to end the SPPD 287(g) ICE partnership — anti-cooperation posture.

profile.religion: NO EVIDENCE FOUND of specific church affiliation; serves on board of and was honored by Earth Mission Ministries (St. Pete Black-church community ministry); former Executive Director, YMCA of Greater St. Petersburg Resource Centers
profile.education: MBA, University of Phoenix (2006); BS Computer Information Systems, Florida A&M (1989, magna cum laude); AA Business Admin., St. Petersburg Junior College (1986); Gibbs Senior HS (1983)
profile.background: City Council, District 5, St. Petersburg — re-elected Nov 5, 2024, sworn for second term Jan 2, 2025; 2024 Council Chair (2023 Vice-Chair). PSTA Chair; Forward Pinellas. President, Personal Agenda LLC; prior at Boley Centers and IBM Global Services; Alpha Kappa Alpha, NAACP.

deborah-figgs-sanders.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 5 St. Petersburg, re-elected Nov 5, 2024, second term to Jan 2029, 2024 Council Chair (stpete.org). HARD evidence: 2024 re-election endorsed by Equality Florida Action PAC (LGBTQ+ advocacy PAC) AND Ruth's List Florida (pro-abortion-rights PAC dedicated to electing Democratic women) — biblical_marriage q0 FALSE, sanctity_of_life q1 FALSE (Florida Politics endorsement roundup). Affirmed transgender community at city Trans Day of Visibility event ('I see you') — biblical_marriage q2 FALSE (Watermark 2024). Participated in city Pride flag raisings (12th annual 2025) and joined Oct 2025 majority moving the Harmony/History LGBTQ+ flag variation forward — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (Watermark + WUSF). Voted YES on FY26 St. Pete budget with 6.4525 millage (3.93% above rollback, ~$1.18B); only Driscoll and Givens dissented — economic_stewardship q2 FALSE (St. Pete Catalyst). Pattern of city Pride/TDOV recognition extends to SOGI cells — family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE. Leads 'Enough is Enough' / 'Hidden Voices' gun-violence response built explicitly on the public-health intervention model (mental health, job programs, returning-citizens outreach) — public_justice q3 FALSE, self_defense q1 FALSE. Suncoast PBA endorsed her 2024 OPPONENT (Torrie Jasuwan), undercutting any affirmative police-partnership claim above routine — public_justice q0 null. NO direct evidence on personhood (q0), election administration, immigration, 2A constitutional carry, or refuse-state-overreach in the conservative-rubric sense — those cells null. (Her LGBTQ-flag majority vote is a STATE-preemption pushback but directionally leftward; refuse_state_overreach q0 null on the conservative-rubric reading.)

1.note: 2024 re-election endorsed by Ruth's List Florida — PAC dedicated to electing pro-abortion-rights Democratic women.

0.note: 2024 re-election endorsed by Equality Florida Action PAC (LGBTQ+ advocacy PAC requiring pro-LGBTQ candidate platform).

2.note: Affirmatively addressed the transgender community at the city's Trans Day of Visibility event ('I see you').

4.note: Participated in city Pride flag raisings; joined Oct 2025 majority advancing the Harmony (Pride) and History flag resolution.

2.note: Pattern of city SOGI affirmation (TDOV public participation, Pride flag) — opposite of opposing CRT/SOGI categories.
Voted YES on the FY2025-26 St. Petersburg budget and 6.4525 millage rate (3.93% above rollback) — only Driscoll and Givens dissented.

3.note: Leads 'Enough is Enough' / 'Hidden Voices' gun-violence response built explicitly on the public-health intervention model (mental health, job programs, returning-citizens outreach).

1.note: Leadership of 'Enough is Enough' gun-violence campaign aligns with the gun-restriction / public-health policy frame.

profile.education: University of Tampa and University of Central Florida (no degree noted); Pasco HS, Dade City FL
profile.background: City Council, District 6, St. Petersburg — elected Nov 2017, re-elected Nov 2021; term expires Jan 2027 (term-limited under St. Pete two-consecutive-term charter). 2020 + 2021 Vice Chair; 2022 Chair of Council. Currently chair of Economic & Workforce Development Committee and Forward Pinellas. Career: Sales & Marketing Manager Hampton Inn & Suites Downtown St. Pete; Account Executive iHeart Media and Tampa Bay Times; Group Sales Manager Don CeSar Hotel. Past president, St. Pete Downtown Neighborhood Association.

gina-driscoll.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: District 6 St. Petersburg, term expires Jan 2027 (two-term limit; stpete.org). HARD evidence: voted YES in committee to advance a $50K grant to the Tampa Bay Abortion Fund + reproductive-health resolution (Creative Loafing Tampa) — sanctity_of_life q0/q1 FALSE; later voted no at full council citing litigation risk under FL law, not pro-life conviction ('a risk I'm not willing to take when we have other ways to reach the same goal,' Tampa Bay Times). Endorsed by Equality Florida Action PAC, sponsored creation of city LGBTQ Small Business Enterprise category — biblical_marriage q0 FALSE (Watermark, Florida Politics). SPONSORED the 2025 Harmony Flag / Pride city-flag resolution to fly Pride colors on city property despite state DEI preemption (WFLA, WUSF) — biblical_marriage q2 FALSE. Publicly defended adult drag performance at Mahaffey Theater ('It's an art form … I've always enjoyed drag shows') — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (Tampa Bay Times via Yahoo). Equality-FL endorsement + drag-access defense extend to family_child_sovereignty q2 FALSE. As 2022 Council Chair defended the FY23 budget that carried a ~13% effective tax increase (~$20.5M more revenue, St. Pete Catalyst) — economic_stewardship q2 FALSE. PLAINTIFF with City of St. Petersburg in lawsuit against state firearm-preemption penalties — self_defense q0 FALSE (Florida Politics). 2025 budget no-vote was a MORE-spending dissent (Fire Rescue), not fiscal restraint. NO documented direct positions on schools curriculum/notification, election administration, immigration enforcement, christian-liberty cells, or public_justice cells (homeless-camping ordinance does not satisfy 'magistrate's sword' framing) — those cells null. NO church/faith affiliation found in any source.

0.note: Voted YES in committee to advance a $50K grant to the Tampa Bay Abortion Fund + reproductive-health resolution; affirmative pro-abortion-access posture.

1.note: At full council, no-vote on the abortion-travel fund was framed as litigation-risk avoidance ('we have other ways to reach the same goal'), not pro-life conviction.

0.note: Endorsed by Equality Florida Action PAC; sponsored creation of city LGBTQ Small Business Enterprise category.

2.note: Sponsored the 2025 'Harmony Flag' / Pride city-flag resolution to fly Pride colors on city property in response to DeSantis-era mural erasures.

4.note: Publicly defended adult drag performance at Mahaffey Theater: 'It's an art form … I've always enjoyed drag shows. It's a unique and wonderful form of performance art.'

2.note: Equality Florida PAC endorsement + public support for all-ages drag access points away from opposing SOGI in K-12 / public programming.
As 2022 Council Chair, defended the FY23 budget carrying a ~13% effective property-tax increase over the rolled-back rate (~$20.5M additional revenue).

0.note: Plaintiff with City of St. Petersburg in lawsuit against state firearm-preemption penalty provisions.

profile.religion: Pentecostal Church of God in Christ (COGIC) family heritage — uncle Clarence pastored Prayer Tower COGIC in the Gas Plant District; father David sang in the choir and taught Sunday school there. Publicly cites 'pray, plan, prepare, perform' as his four guiding principles (prayer listed first).
profile.education: MBA, Florida A&M University; BA Accounting, University of South Florida
profile.background: 54th Mayor of St. Petersburg, first elected Nov 2021 (St. Pete's first Black mayor), sworn Jan 6, 2022; running for re-election in the Aug 18, 2026 primary (Nov 3, 2026 runoff if needed). Prior: Pinellas County Commissioner 2000-2022 (District 7). Accountant at Florida Power Corporation. Third-generation St. Pete native (b. Aug 15, 1964). Wife Donna, two daughters.

kenneth-t-welch.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: 54th Mayor of St. Petersburg, sworn Jan 6, 2022; declared candidate in Aug 18, 2026 primary (Wikipedia 2026 mayoral race page). March 2026 polling showed Welch (19%) trailing Charlie Crist (23%) in a 7-way race incl. Brandi Gabbard, Jim Large (R), Maria Scruggs, Kevin Batdorf, Paul Congemi. HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION: Welch openly identifies with his family's COGIC heritage and publicly cites 'pray, plan, prepare, perform' as his guiding principles (prayer first) — christian_liberty q0 TRUE (Tampa Magazines long-form interview). HARD evidence on LGBTQ+ cells: personally raises the Pride flag over City Hall every year (12th annual 2025); 2022 proclamation: 'We cannot and will not stand silent while our fundamental rights of freedom, choice and self-determination are stripped by anyone … including politicians in Washington and Tallahassee'; 2026 wrapped City Hall steps in Pride colors and pledged ~$300K-equivalent in-kind public-safety support to St. Pete Pride despite SB 1134 (St. Pete Catalyst, Florida Politics, CL Tampa) — biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE. AFFIRMATIVE policing posture: $171M SPPD budget, body cams/tasers/drones, public partnership with Chief Holloway, publicly thanked Rep. Luna for $480K in federal cruiser funding ('This kind of support means a lot … setting a national standard') — public_justice q0 TRUE (St. Pete Catalyst, St Pete Rising). HIGH-VALUE DEVIATION: maintains SPPD's 287(g) ICE partnership and has REFUSED activist pressure to cancel it ('Mayor Ken Welch has the capacity to get rid of the 287(g) agreement, but he is actively choosing not to do so' — Florida Phoenix / WMNF); St. Pete is NOT a sanctuary city — border_immigration q2 TRUE. ECON: FY23 budget approved with ~13% effective property-tax increase (~$20.5M more revenue, St. Pete Catalyst); Welch publicly blasted Crist for proposing tax cuts — economic_stewardship q2 FALSE. ABORTION: administration raised 'process and precedent' concerns about the Tampa Bay Abortion Fund grant proposal (council killed 6-2); no affirmative pro-life position either — sanctity_of_life cells null. STATE PUSHBACK: led FL mayors against SB 1134 anti-DEI bill and defied it on Pride; this is local-control posture but directionally leftward (defending DEI/Pride), so refuse_state_overreach cells null under the conservative-rubric reading. NO direct evidence on schools (mayor lane), election administration, christian_liberty q2-q4, public_justice q1-q4, or self-defense (FL preemption blocks municipal gun regs) — those cells null.

0.note: Annual Pride flag-raising over City Hall + 'committed to equity and diversity in all of our policies and decisions' proclamation; no affirmation of one-man/one-woman marriage in the public record.

2.note: In 2026 wrapped City Hall steps in Pride colors despite SB 1134; pledged ~$300K-equivalent in-kind city support to St. Pete Pride.

4.note: Personally raised the Pride flag every year of his tenure (12th annual 2025); 2022 proclamation: 'We cannot and will not stand silent…'

0.note: Publicly cites 'pray, plan, prepare, perform' as his four guiding principles (prayer first) and openly references his family's COGIC church heritage (Prayer Tower COGIC, Gas Plant District) — affirmative public profession of Christian faith.

2.note: FY23 budget approved at a millage rate characterized as ~13% increase over rolled-back rate (~$20.5M additional revenue); Welch publicly blasted opponent Crist for proposing tax cuts.

0.note: Affirmative SPPD partnership: $171M public-safety line; body cams/tasers/drones; publicly thanked Rep. Luna for $480K federal cruiser funding ('setting a national standard'); ongoing public partnership with Chief Holloway.

2.note: Maintains SPPD's 287(g) ICE partnership and has refused activist pressure to cancel it; St. Pete is not a sanctuary city — functional opposition to sanctuary policy.
